preamble: The retraction and fortition of the labio-velar glide [w] to a voiced uvular stop [ɢ] is a specialized shift. It occurs in certain Arabic loanword adaptations into languages with back-consonant systems, where the velar component of the glide is retracted and strengthened.

examples.note: Retraction of the velar component of /w/ in back-consonant heavy phonologies.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
Watson, J. C. (2002). The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic.
